channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-30
item.description:
Our community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects recently. The `giladfeldman/docpluck` project has released a new version, `v2.4.99`, with notable updates including a fix for stale PDF extractor paths and a new feature for region-driven Camelot capture. Meanwhile, `JamieCummins/regcheck` has made significant strides in hardening multi-study extractions and improving report text normalization, with a revamped report render and updated UI output. Connections between projects are also being strengthened, with `giladfeldman/docpluck` targeting data processing goals (DP-1 and DP-2) that may benefit from the enhanced report generation capabilities in `regcheck`. Additionally, smaller but important updates have been made to `shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve` and `giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward`, including a revised validation note with citation and a new Chrome Webstore link, respectively. Overall, our community is celebrating a period of active development and collaboration.

35 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

Most active:

regcheck14 commits
metacheck10 commits
docpluck7 commits
escicheck4 commits

item.title: docpluck: 3 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, where a version bump to `v2.4.99` was made, along with a `CHANGELOG` update, as part of a "chore(release)" effort. Additionally on the `main` branch, a fix was applied to correct "stale PDFextractor/docpluck paths" which were updated to point to the "MetaScienceTools/" layout, addressing an issue with skills functionality. The `main` branch also saw the addition of a new feature for "region-driven Camelot capture" with "content-plausibility guards" for tables, as described in the "feat(tables)" commit, which targets DP-1 and DP-2.

3 commit(s) today:

  • 03e5d55 [main] chore(release): v2.4.99 — region-driven table capture (version bump + CHANGELOG) — Gilad Feldman
  • b0fdd5f [main] fix(skills): correct stale PDFextractor/docpluck paths to MetaScienceTools/ layout — Gilad Feldman
  • fd072b8 [main] feat(tables): region-driven Camelot capture + content-plausibility guards (DP-1/DP-2) — Gilad Feldman
  • b6bd65e [main] fix(tables): v2.4.98 — DP-3 η²p-from-structure, DP-5 r-cell + bracket-CI (ESCIcheck handoff; flatten-only, AI-gold-verified) — Gilad Feldman
  • 521089a [main] feat(tables): RC-T char-level column-detection fallback for tight-kerned tables (v2.4.98, foundation) — Gilad Feldman
  • 976af30 [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-25 run handoff + LEARNINGS + project lessons (PARTIAL) — Gilad Feldman
  • 59cff5b [main] fix(tooling): direction-aware check_app_pin_sync note + RC-T/RC-1 spec findings — Gilad Feldman
  • 3e9cc2c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(learnings): RC-T Layer-2 + combined-ship closeout (cycle 2, v2.4.97) — Gilad Feldman
  • 813aa4c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] fix(tables): v2.4.97 — DP-2 blank p/df + DP-5 two-header-row recovery/super-header alignment + RC-T Layer-2 raw_text prose-trim/suppress — Gilad Feldman
  • 84a4d42 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] fix(render): RC-T — strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose (v2.4.96) — Gilad Feldman
  • 7247285 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(handoff+todo): RC-T implementation handoff + log RC-T deferral / close collabra.77859 adjudication — Gilad Feldman
  • 927d869 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(spec): RC-T table-region prose-contamination — confirmed 2-layer root cause + fix plan — Gilad Feldman
  • db7192b [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(triage+learnings): v2.4.95 corpus assessment — 7/7 canary FAIL, RC-B7 already done (W0h), RC-T root-caused — Gilad Feldman
  • 2dbdd98 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs+tooling: enforce library↔app version-pin sync (directive + qa/review/deploy gate) — Gilad Feldman
  • c1fa68b [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(todo): log v2.4.95 / REQUEST_11 shipped + deferred follow-ups (effect_type opt-in, 77859 caption-number, text-layer caveat) — Gilad Feldman
  • 370b89c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(handoff): record the cross-session skill-file commit coordination (now fulfilled in b2c6aa2) — Gilad Feldman
  • b2c6aa2 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] chore(docs+deploy): prior-session housekeeping — REQUEST_10 customer broadcast, daily-digest cron-retry lesson, deploy smoke-gate fix — Gilad Feldman
  • bce4b7f [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] feat(tables): REQUEST_11 — flatten fields for non-clinical result tables (v2.4.95) — Gilad Feldman
  • 83ab6bf [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(deploy): document pre-push canary hook timing — never timeout/kill the push — Gilad Feldman
  • 1158b1b [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] feat(tables): REQUEST_10 — HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes + cross-flavor capture (v2.4.94) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The main branch was the focus of the recent work, with two commits made to it. The first commit, made on June 29, "sync from escicheckapp@26f8430 (effectcheck v0.6.8)" as stated in the message, which indicates that the repository was synced with escicheckapp at the specified commit hash, bringing in effectcheck version 0.6.8. The second commit, made on June 30, "sync from escicheckapp@1d4c2dd (effectcheck v0.6.9)" similarly synced the repository with escicheckapp, this time updating to effectcheck version 0.6.9. No other branches were worked on during this time. These commits brought the repository up to date with the latest versions of effectcheck, specifically versions 0.6.8 and 0.6.9.

2 commit(s) today:

  • cc5cfd0 [main] sync from escicheckapp@1d4c2dd (effectcheck v0.6.9) (#23) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →
